objective: Implement and prove a real single-use Firecracker lifecycle with pinned artifacts, nonce-bound vsock handshake, post-admission fresh assignment, bounded resources, measured readiness, repeated-run freshness, forced-timeout cleanup, and teardown proof.
assumptions: Setup completes before controller admission; concurrent cache mutation and chamber execution are not admitted.
Cold-boot single-use correctness is required before snapshot or startup optimization.
The Firecracker vsock mapping is the sole Phase 1 guest-host data channel; no TAP device or external forwarding is configured.
The unprivileged Build Target 1 slice runs the pinned Firecracker binary directly; jailer hardening remains explicitly unchecked.
A new writable ext4 copy is created from the hash-checked base image for every admitted run and deleted during teardown.

commands_executed.concise_output: Pinned downloads verified; initial setup stopped because the new rootfs builder lacked executable mode. No VM launched.
Firecracker, kernel, kernel config, static init, and ext4 image built and verified.

commands_executed.command: three retained diagnostic real-lifecycle invocations during implementation
commands_executed.concise_output: Quarantined logs identified distinct local defects: Firecracker instance-ID charset, per-UID NPROC accounting, and HTTP keep-alive framing. Each failed run tore down.

commands_executed.command: real Firecracker lifecycle invocation after bounded fixes
commands_executed.concise_output: Ready in 847 ms; assignment acknowledged; guest rebooted; Firecracker exit 0; PID, sockets, and run directory gone.

commands_executed.command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v
commands_executed.concise_output: 19 tests passed sequentially after one exit-gate repair.
Ready at 854 ms and 860 ms; distinct run IDs, canaries, and challenges; no prior state; both exits 0; both teardown checks true.

tests_and_falsifiers.test: Pinned Firecracker archive, extracted binary, kernel, kernel config, static init, and rootfs hashes
Two rootfs builds separated in time are byte-identical
Two sequential real microVM boots use fresh identities, canaries, challenges, and writable copies
Both real sequential boots report no prior marker before assignment
Invalid resource limits fail before launch
Two-second real VMM timeout forces process termination and removes run state
Fixed lifecycle events have HOST_ENFORCED authority
Guest image contains no API-key-shaped literal, OpenAI credential variable name, or auth.json
Shell syntax, C warnings-as-errors syntax, Python AST, diff whitespace, ext4 integrity, and empty run directory

limitations_and_checks_not_run: The pinned jailer was not used; production namespace/cgroup hardening is not claimed.
Linux RLIMIT_NPROC is per real host UID; the adapter enforces a bounded additional allowance, not a dedicated per-chamber cgroup.
The guest prior-state flag is GUEST_REPORTED; host enforcement independently proves a new rootfs copy and deletion of the run directory.
No live model provider, Codex guest process, target tool, or real user data was present in Phase 1.
No general guest syscall, process, or filesystem-read sensor is claimed.
detected_drift: The first exit-gate orchestration incorrectly ran setup mutation and tests concurrently against one cache. Hash guards rejected transient artifacts; no architecture or trust-boundary change was required.

repair_attempts.original_failure: Exit gate ran setup and lifecycle tests concurrently; kernel hash guard and one Firecracker start rejected transient cache state.
repair_attempts.allowed_scope: Verification ordering only; no product, contract, dependency, or trust-boundary change.
repair_attempts.repair: Completed setup first, verified all artifact hashes, then reran the full suite sequentially and performed read-only close checks.
repair_attempts.retest_command: PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v
